marketState: neutral, pulled between bullish near-term weather/positioning and a bearish storage/trend backdrop
summary: Front price ($2.911) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value (band $2.423-$3.423, spread -$0.012), so there is no clean mispricing edge right now. Seasonality is unhelpful here: price is running -7.7pp below its typical-year path, the 4-week outlook is a weak 38% probability higher with a -1.4% typical move, and reliability is explicitly flagged Low (59pp spread). The strongest fundamental signal is weather - both the 1-7D (+16.8% vs 5y, rangePos 0.75) and 8-16D (+21.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.97, near the top of its range) windows show unusually hot, demand-heavy conditions, which argues for near-term burn support. That bullish weather case is reinforced by extreme managed-money positioning (net short -105,709 contracts, just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years - a classic stretched/contrarian setup), but it fights against a clearly bearish storage picture (3,024 Bcf, +180 Bcf above the 5-year average, and a +22 Bcf bearish surprise build) and bearish higher-timeframe technicals (4h/1d/1w all trending bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses). Net-net, the data doesn't line up cleanly enough for a high-conviction directional call over a multi-week horizon, while the next 1-10 days lean cautiously long on the weather/momentum combination.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (top priority) is weak and explicitly low-reliability, storage is +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish surprise build, and 1d/1w technicals are in a bearish EMA death-cross - none of that supports a long-term long. But hot 1-7D/8-16D weather and an 8th-percentile managed-money short position argue against pressing a short too. The conflicting priority-1 (seasonality) and priority-2 (weather) reads mean there is no edge worth sizing into yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either: (a) a storage report that prints at/below forecast (surprise turns neutral-to-bullish), signaling the surplus is stabilizing, or (b) 1d/1w trend flipping bullish (50>200 EMA cross, RSI back above 50) confirming COT short-covering is actually showing up in price, or (c) a pullback into the lower half of the fair-value band (roughly $2.60-$2.75) that would offer a better risk/reward long entry alongside the already-stretched short COT positioning.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (keep size small given conflicting higher-timeframe technicals)
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather is genuinely hot on both windows (1-7D +16.8% vs 5y, 8-16D +21.5% vs 5y and rangePos 0.97 near the top of its range), which supports near-term gas burn, and shorter-timeframe technicals (15m bullish, 1h RSI 58 with positive MACD) show early upward momentum even though 4h/1d remain bearish. Price sitting right at fair value means this is a momentum/weather trade, not a value trade, so risk is kept tight.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back below $2.78 or a fade in the 8-16D demand anomaly (pctVs5y/rangePos rolling over) would invalidate the near-term bullish case.

keyDrivers: 1-7D and 8-16D demand both well above 5-year norms (+16.8% / +21.5%), with the 8-16D window at rangePos 0.97 (near the top of its range)
Managed money net short -105,709 contracts, only the 8th percentile of the last 5 years - a stretched, potentially squeeze-prone position
Storage at 3,024 Bcf, +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise build, capping upside
Seasonality reliability flagged Low (59pp 10-90% spread) with only 38% probability of higher prices 3-4 weeks out and a -1.4% typical 4-week move
Higher-timeframe technicals (4h/1d/1w) all show bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, while price sits almost exactly at modeled fair value
risks: Seasonality's own reliability metric is Low this week (59pp spread), so the historical edge behind any seasonal read is weak
Storage surplus could keep pressuring price if summer heat fails to draw the +180 Bcf overhang down toward the 5y average
Extreme managed-money short positioning can persist or extend further before any short-covering materializes, so treat it as a background tailwind, not a timing signal
